% Abstract — auto-generated by report/paper_sections.py (honest, data-driven)
\begin{abstract}
We present a Hybrid Quantum-Classical Transformer (HQCT) for clinical risk
stratification that integrates a variational quantum circuit (VQC) into the
feed-forward sublayer of a TabTransformer architecture. To match circuit
capacity to the available data, the VQC complexity is selected adaptively per
training fold (4--6 qubits, 2--3 layers of a Hardware-Efficient Ansatz with
data re-uploading). We evaluate HQCT on 4 clinically validated benchmarks
(CKD, FHS, PIMA, and Cleveland) using 10-fold stratified cross-validation with SMOTE confined to
training folds, against XGBoost, LightGBM, TabTransformer, and MLP baselines.
HQCT ranks among the top two models by F1-score on FHS and Cleveland, matching the strongest classical baselines, while trailing the best classical model on the near-saturated CKD and PIMA benchmarks (CKD: 98.25\% accuracy, F1 98.58\%, ROC-AUC 0.9963; FHS: 78.33\% accuracy, F1 31.57\%, ROC-AUC 0.6800; PIMA: 74.20\% accuracy, F1 65.42\%, ROC-AUC 0.8132; Cleveland: 83.15\% accuracy, F1 81.13\%, ROC-AUC 0.9096). Notably, HQCT attains the lowest cross-validation variance on Cleveland, indicating more stable generalisation under limited clinical data. The Framingham (FHS) benchmark is deliberately challenging (severe class imbalance, $\sim$15\% positive rate); no model exceeds F1$\,\approx\,$35\%, and within this constrained band HQCT ranks 2nd of 5 by F1 -- honest evidence on a dataset where no method attains clinical-grade performance.
Statistical comparisons use McNemar's exact test, Wilcoxon signed-rank, and
DeLong AUC tests; we report effect sizes and bootstrap confidence intervals
rather than significance alone. Circuit characterisation gives a Meyer--Wallach expressibility of 1.000 and entanglement capability 0.948 for the 6-qubit, 3-layer ansatz.
To support trustworthy clinical deployment, we add SHA-256 cryptographic
provenance for all artifacts, SHAP and quantum gradient feature attribution,
calibration analysis, and optional differential-privacy training (DP-SGD).
All code, data hashes, and reproducibility scripts are provided at
\url{[repository\_url]}.
\end{abstract}
